candidates.title: Post-Release Hygiene Consolidation
candidates.summary: Make v0.6.12-post-release-evidence the canonical record; tighten the v0.6.13 candidate-selection gate; add deterministic checker/test coverage.
candidates.user_value: Reviewers have a single canonical evidence record and a clearer selection gate.
candidates.safety_boundary: Docs/checker/test only; no runtime or boundary changes.

candidates.title: Bounded Autonomy Governance
candidates.summary: Create docs/bounded-live-autonomy-governance.md and align docs/autonomy-roadmap.md with staged autonomy levels and hard invariants.
candidates.user_value: Reviewers can verify the project's conservative autonomy stance and governance ladder.
candidates.safety_boundary: Governance docs only; no runtime or boundary changes.
candidates.ranking_reason: Documents existing safety posture; low risk.

candidates.title: Autonomous Paper Workflow Demo and Evidence Gate
candidates.user_value: Reviewers can run and verify a deterministic L1 paper-only autonomy demo locally.
candidates.safety_boundary: Docs/checker/test only; paper-only, offline, no credentials; no runtime or boundary changes.
candidates.ranking_reason: Concrete L1 autonomy proof without live-trading claims; low risk.

candidates.title: Paper Mode Offline Provider Isolation and No-Network Gate
candidates.summary: Wire NullProvider into the provider factory; add paper-mode missing-credential fallback and explicit --offline flag; add doc, checker, tests, demo update, and cross-doc/gate integration.
candidates.user_value: Paper agentic workflows can run without provider credentials or network; live mode stays fail-closed.
candidates.safety_boundary: Provider/CLI/runner wiring only; no broker, execution, safety, risk, or config boundary changes.
candidates.ranking_reason: Removes the local provider transport failure blocker for paper mode while preserving live fail-closed behavior.

candidates.title: Autonomous Paper Strategy Evaluation Matrix and Promotion Gate
candidates.summary: Add atlas backtest compare, paper-only evaluation artifacts, demo, checker, tests, and cross-doc/gate integration.
candidates.user_value: Reviewers can compare sample-data strategy behavior and see paper-only candidate/reject/needs-more-testing decisions.
candidates.safety_boundary: Backtest/CLI/reporting/docs/checker/test only; no provider, broker, live, safety, risk, or config boundary changes.
candidates.ranking_reason: Adds conservative paper research gating without live-promotion claims; low risk.

candidates.title: Paper Strategy Sensitivity Matrix and Deterministic Fixture

rejected.title: Live trading default changes
rejected.reason: Live trading must remain disabled by default; requires explicit opt-in and owner approval.

rejected.title: Broker execution enablement
rejected.reason: Broker order submission remains blocked for non-paper brokers; no adapter or execution changes.

rejected.title: Provider execution enablement
rejected.reason: Provider execution remains locked by default; no real provider calls in CI or demo.

rejected.title: Autonomous live trading
rejected.reason: Autonomous live trading is not a current capability or milestone; requires external gates.

rejected.title: Version bump to 0.6.13
rejected.reason: Source/package version must remain 0.6.12 until release prep explicitly starts.

rejected.title: v0.6.13 tag/release/PyPI
rejected.reason: No annotated tag, GitHub Release, or PyPI publication for v0.6.13 without owner-approved release prep.

safety_boundaries: Human approval required for live order flow.
No credentials, secrets, or API keys committed.
No real broker/provider calls in CI, tests, or demo.
constraints: Source/package version must remain 0.6.12 until release prep starts.
PyPI must remain disabled unless explicitly approved.
No v0.6.13 tag or GitHub Release may be created from planning candidates.
Every candidate must have deterministic checker and/or test coverage.
Local gates and CI quick-gate must pass before a candidate is complete.
release_criteria: All accepted candidates implemented, tested, and documented.
No protected runtime boundary changes unless separately approved.
